creator: pav@FreeBSD.org
text:
> |> As for the 1551 ports that are installed into X11BASE, I believe that a
> |> gradual move before XOrg would be beneficial, otherwise these ports will
> |> be BROKEN when Xorg is moved, and cause us to rush out patches to get
> |
> | I don't think there will be any rush, why do you believe that? The apps
> | can live in /usr/X11R6 or any other prefix as long as they want to.

> There will be a need to rush out changes once XOrg is moved, otherwise
> these specific files (app-defaults, fonts) will not function properly. I
> would rather see these things stand out so they do not get overlooked.
> By having them in X_PLIST_*, a simple grep will show which ports need to
> be modified once XOrg is moved, and we wont be digging through
> pkg-plist's to find them.

Good analyze of problem, bad solution.

All the ports their plists are tied to Xorg must be moved at the same
moment as Xorg. There must be no slow transition, no window of breakage.

Note that the same was done recently for GNOME, where ~100 non-GNOME
apps was moved to /usr/local because they installed files into "special"
GNOME locations.

So it surely can be done.

I'd say the task here is to identify such ports and preparing the
patches, coordinated with Xorg upgrade effort.

But I don't think we need to mark those ports by some special markings.
The list can be kept separate.

-- 
Pav Lucistnik <pav@oook.cz>
              <pav@FreeBSD.org>

What do you mean? An African or a European swallow?
